IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Monday, July 20, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — The Midwest's hottest board right now. Forty-three calls, one-eighty-four hands deep, but the real money's at the data centers: Meade Electric's Meta facility in Lebanon is running **$53.80/hr plus 10% over scale** on 5-12s, Mon-Fri with Saturday work. Fluor's Lilly job rolls pension benefits on top. Ermco's Google data center work includes OSHA 10 requirement but no per diem hit. Industrial Electric's indoor AC retrofit at IU Hospital is steadier 5-10s if you want a break from the DC grind.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Port Washington Data Center is a repost machine eating bodies. Three contractors (Gurtz, MJ, Cupertino) posting twenty-six, twenty, and eighteen calls each on the same job — **JW wage plus $10/hr base wage** is the standard across all three. Pieper's holding eleven calls on healthcare work in Milwaukee proper. This board moves fast; if you're seeing reposts, the job's staffed quick.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — Twenty-four calls, eighty-five hands, but the per diem is fat. Parsons Electric's dominating the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood with forty-one total calls at **$44.80/hr plus $100/day non-taxed per diem** Mon-Fri, bumps to **$150 on weekends**, and another $100 incentive if you carry ND reciprocal. Six-10s. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base work at same rates, 4-10s. You're making $65+ all-in with incentives.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** — Micron's massive. VECA and Quality Electric are stacked on the same ID1 project: **$45.33/hr plus $170/day per diem for seven days** if eligible, plus a **$20/hr incentive**. That's real money even below scale. Four-10s, day shift starts 6am.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** — Twenty-one calls, ninety-five hands. Conti's 5C Data Center in Springfield is the outlier at **$56.07/hr**, but you need MV splicing certification. Mid City's various locations sit at $46. Cleaner board than the eastern mega-locals.

The data center boom is sorting the haves from the have-nots — if you're not chasing DC work with per diem incentives, you're leaving three grand a month on the table.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Monday, July 20, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is running hot with 45 calls and a tight **120-hand book**. NEW RIVER ELECTRIC is hammering distribution work across Columbus and Southern Ohio—**$125/night per diem**, $100 bonus if you work every available day, CDL required. THAYER POWER is also moving pole replacement (paid hotels) and transmission gigs with bonus tiers running $150–$600 depending on location and experience. This is straight distro and transmission grind, but the volume and bonus structure make it worth the move.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** has 24 calls with a deep book at 100 hands. VECA Electric and Cochran are staffing the Micron fab build—**$45.33/hr base** with **$170/day per diem** (7 days if eligible) and travel pay bumping it to **$20/hr extra**. Yeah, it's below scale, but the per diem math is sticky and the PLA work is steady. Orientation starts 7/21; expect 4–6 month runway.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is quieter at 24 calls but aggressive. Parsons Electric is driving the Polaris Forge Data Center build in Harwood—**$44.8/hr**, 6–10s schedule with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** plus **$150 Sat/Sun incentive**. It's inside work, heavy hours, but non-taxed money moves the needle.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** shows 11 calls with solid scale: Summit and Cupertino are pulling **$55/hr all-OT double time** on inside projects (NM JIW license EE98J required). B&D wants fire alarm/VESDA background at the same rate.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** has ArchKey running 45 hands on the Google Data Center in West Memphis, AR—**$36.65/hr** plus **$125/day per diem incentive** for full shift, 4/10s and 2/8s. Low scale but volume is there.

Data-center work is pulling hands out of traditional transmission and distro—the money's lower but guaranteed steady hours and per diem stacks.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Monday, July 20, 2026**

**LU-140 Salt Lake City, UT** is running hot with 24 calls and a **$6.00 scale**. BCS Mechanical alone is posting 31 journeyman plumber slots on the West Valley build, while ACCO's Cedar City gig adds **$175/day per diem** for full-week commitments—that's real money on top of scale. DSI's Draper shop work needs TIG tackers; apprentices have runway too. Salt Lake's got 79 hands available but the work's spreading faster than bodies can fill it.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** shows 23 calls chasing 51 hands. US Eng. Metalworks' shop fitter calls pay **15% shift differential**, Kiewit's Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse run includes **$65/day per diem** with industrial wage rates, and Murphy's fab shop (4pm start, shift diff) specifically wants guys who can tack weld. The dayshift/nightshift splits at Kiewit mean flexibility if you can swing it.

**LU-776 Lima, OH** is posting **$53.00/hr scale**—top end in this batch. Graham's Cenovus maintenance gig runs 5-8s with spotty OT and requires current certifications. The ethanol plant combo welder slot hits **$53 + Toledo rate**, and Nooter's fab shop runs **$125/day per diem** on an incentive structure. Three locals are actually competitive here.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** has MAP posting 12 calls at Holly Frontier Sinclair refinery—day shift, 7-10s, welders and fitters. PMI's base add **$100/day per diem** (days worked) across Ellsworth AFB and Fort Meade VA Hospital gigs in South Dakota. Background checks required; OSHA 10 on the hospital run is mandatory.

**LU-290 Portland, OR** and **LU-525 Las Vegas, NV** are tight boards (24 and 18 hands respectively) but specialty-heavy: Portland's hitting Intel projects with BIM detailers and refrigeration techs; Vegas is all plumber calls at the Strip properties with Hansen Mech and Bombard rotating crews.

The refinery and power plant maintenance shutdowns are pulling hands southeast and north; hospitality and semiconductor projects are holding the coasts steady.
